definition: Use transformations, norm-range partitioning, asymmetric hashing, clustering, or quantization designed specifically for maximum inner-product search when dot product is the scientific similarity. This avoids incorrectly normalizing away vector norm when norm carries scientific meaning.

scale_profile.best_for: Large vector audits where the chosen similarity is dot product or score compatibility rather than cosine or Euclidean distance.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions to billions of vectors with approximate hashing, clustering, graph, or quantized indexes.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Vectors are intentionally unit-normalized and cosine similarity is the actual scientific definition.

tunable_parameters.name: transform_family
tunable_parameters.purpose: Selects ALSH, norm partitioning, quantization, clustering, or sparse MIPS indexing.

tunable_parameters.name: norm_partition_count
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls norm buckets when norm-range methods are used.

quality_gates: Check whether norm is scientifically meaningful before converting MIPS to cosine search.
Measure recall@k against exact inner products on a subset.
Report vector normalization, norm partitioning, and any asymmetric transform.
failure_modes: Cosine normalization can erase meaningful magnitude information.
Transforms may have weak recall for unusual vector norm distributions.
Sparse and dense inner-product indexes have different failure modes and should not be swapped silently.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py with dot-product scoring as an exact reference; production MIPS systems should emit the same pairwise columns and report the transform used.

citations.title: Asymmetric LSH for Sublinear Time Maximum Inner Product Search
citations.evidence: Introduces asymmetric locality-sensitive hashing to reduce MIPS to an approximate near-neighbor problem.

citations.title: Norm-Range Partition: A Universal Catalyst for LSH based Maximum Inner Product Search
citations.evidence: Partitions vectors by norm to improve LSH-based MIPS efficiency.

citations.title: Quantization based Fast Inner Product Search
citations.evidence: Uses quantization to accelerate approximate inner-product search.
